issues.current_text: the value of `x` converted to the wider numeric type
issues.problem: The Scaladoc is attached to a comment block placed above an `import` statement (line 649) rather than directly above the four implicit conversion defs. As written, the @param and @return tags do not actually document any particular method — they describe the group of conversions conceptually. This is a pre-existing structural issue, not something this change introduced, but the @return text is accurate and reasonable for the conceptual group it describes.
issues.suggestion: the value of `x` converted to the wider numeric type

general_feedback: The single change replaces the `TODO FILL IN` placeholder for the @return tag on the shared Scaladoc block describing the implicit `short2int`/`short2long`/`short2float`/`short2double` conversions. The new text — 'the value of `x` converted to the wider numeric type' — is accurate, concise, follows Scaladoc conventions (lowercase, backticked code reference), and is consistent in style with the surrounding @param. It correctly describes what all four conversions return without overspecifying a particular target type, which is appropriate since the block documents the group of widening coercions collectively.
